reviewNotes: HOLD - not publish-ready. HMTU (Truong Dai hoc Ky thuat Y te Hai Duong) is a Vietnamese Ministry of Health public university training Vietnamese-medium nursing, midwifery, medical laboratory technology, medical imaging technology, physiotherapy and (per some secondary listings) general medicine staff for Vietnam's domestic health system. Multi-source web research (official site hmtu.edu.vn, MOET recognised-institutions list, studyinvietnam.edu.vn listing, university.me, WholeNurseCatalog, general search aggregation) found NO evidence of: (1) an English-medium program of any kind, (2) an international/overseas-student admissions track, (3) any officialProgramUrl for a courseSlug in {mbbs, medical-pg, bsc-nursing, pharmacy, bds} with confirmed medium of instruction and duration, or (4) any NMC/PCI/INC-relevant recognition statement for Indian students. The official homepage and program pages are Vietnamese-only; no /en/ English section could be found or fetched (attempts to hmtu.edu.vn and legacy www.hmtu.edu.vn/Desktop.aspx paths were tried; the legacy path 404s). Per the sourcing rules (exhaustive-then-omit, never fabricate), no structuredFacts.programs entry can be honestly populated with the required officialProgramUrl/medium/durationYears for an India-audience course slug, so this draft cannot pass the publish gate. Held as a local research record only; do not seed or publish. Revisit if/when HMTU publishes an English-medium admissions track for international students.

sources.label: HMTU official website (Vietnamese, home page)
sources.notes: Fetched via curl. No English-language section found; no international/foreign-student admissions content found.

sources.label: HMTU legacy site introduction page (404, could not verify)
sources.notes: Legacy domain path returned HTTP 404 Not Found on fetch; used only to corroborate the current domain is hmtu.edu.vn.

sources.label: Vietnam Ministry of Education and Training (MOET) - recognised higher education institutions list
sources.notes: Lists HMTU among MOET-recognised institutions; used to corroborate public/government status.

sources.label: studyinvietnam.edu.vn - Hai Duong Medical Technical University profile
sources.notes: Surfaced in search results describing HMTU's founding in 1960 and 2007 university upgrade; direct fetch of this URL failed (connection error), so content is corroborated only via search-result summary, not a direct page read.

sources.label: university.me - HMTU programs listing
sources.notes: Aggregator listing of HMTU programs; fetched via curl but contained no explicit English-medium or international-student program details usable for structuredFacts.programs.

sources.label: WholeNurseCatalog - HMTU listing
sources.notes: Corroborates HMTU's public, Ministry of Health-governed status and its nursing/medical-technology training mission; surfaced via search only.

structuredFacts.name: Hai Duong Medical Technical University
structuredFacts.notes: establishedYear=1960 reflects the institution's founding as a Secondary Medical Technical School; it was upgraded to full university status in 2007 by Prime Ministerial decision (both facts corroborated across search summaries of the official site and MOET-adjacent listings). No programs array populated: no courseSlug-eligible program (mbbs/medical-pg/bsc-nursing/pharmacy/bds) could be corroborated with an explicit officialProgramUrl, medium of instruction, and duration for an English-medium or international-student track. bestFitFor intentionally left empty rather than fabricated, since no India-audience-relevant program is confirmed.

draftContent.notes: All narrative fields intentionally left empty. HMTU is a Vietnamese Ministry of Health institution training Vietnamese-medium nursing/medical-technology staff for the domestic workforce; no corroborated English-medium program or international-admissions pathway exists for Indian students, so writing India-audience narrative content (hostel life, Indian food support, clinical exposure framed for foreign MBBS/nursing students, etc.) would require fabrication, which is disallowed. This page should not be published until/unless HMTU is confirmed to run an English-medium program open to international/Indian applicants.
